questions.question:
Let \( m \) and \( n \) be any two positive integers. Then, which of the following is FALSE?



Combinatory: TIFR CSE 2014 | Part A | Question: 14
options: If one of \( m \), \( n \) is prime, then there are integers \( x, y \) such that \( mx + ny = 1 \).
If \( m < n \), then \( m! \) divides \( n(n − 1)(n − 2) \ldots (n − m + 1) \).
If \( 2^{n} − 1 \) is prime, then \( n \) is prime.
questions.correct_answer: If one of \( m \), \( n \) is prime, then there are integers \( x, y \) such that \( mx + ny = 1 \).
